title: Quantum Computing Methods Overcome Hardware Limits With Hybrid Classical Approaches
why:
- Researchers benchmarked recent developments in the Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE) algorithm, which uses a hybrid quantum-classical approach to overcome the limitations of current Noisy Intermediate Scale Quantum (NISQ) hardware.
- Different adaptations of VQE, such as circuit complexity reduction and chemistry-inspired ansatzes, were developed to improve performance and scalability on available quantum processors.
- A 72-qubit superconducting processor was used to implement the VQE algorithm, demonstrating how hybrid strategies can delegate tasks optimally between quantum and classical computation.
- The study extended VQE to include excited state calculations, broadening its practical application in quantum chemistry and materials science.
- Current quantum machines have up to 6,000 physical qubits, but the research highlights the need for millions of high-fidelity qubits for fault-tolerant, large-scale problem solving.
- Benchmarking of quantum algorithms was rigorously performed using classical quantum simulators, providing critical validation and insights into algorithmic performance.
